_note: English source of truth for the /reading page. Every language file (reading.<code>.json) overrides these keys and deep-merges over English, so missing keys fall back here. Content is Jyotish tradition, offered for reflection — never a prediction, medical/financial claim, or guarantee. Proper nouns (rashi/graha) are romanized Sanskrit in English; translations put them in native script under names.*. Interpolating sentences live in tpl.* with {named} tokens the renderer fills (values may already carry <b> markup).

ui.title: Your birth chart
ui.lead: Enter the exact moment and place you were born and we'll cast your sidereal (Lahiri) chart — your rising sign, your houses, every graha to the degree. The astronomy is exact; the tradition is labelled as tradition.
ui.unknownLabel: I don't know my birth time
ui.timeHint: Even a few minutes changes your chart — the rising sign moves about one sign every two hours.
ui.disclaimer: How to read this. Positions marked “Computed fact” come from NASA-grade ephemeris and are astronomically checkable. Anything marked “Jyotish tradition” is a traditional interpretation offered for reflection and cultural heritage — not a prediction, and not a substitute for professional medical, legal or financial advice. No outcome is guaranteed; your decisions are your own.
ui.errDate: Please enter your date of birth.
ui.errPlace: Please pick your birthplace from the list of Indian cities.
ui.errRange: Janam Express covers births from 1950 to 2010.
ui.errTime: Enter your birth time, or tick “I don't know my birth time”.
ui.errSnag: The sky-almanac hit a snag — please try again.
ui.previewUnknown: Birth time unknown — the ascendant needs a time.
ui.previewEnter: Enter date, place and time to see your rising sign.
ui.secDasha: The planetary period you're living through
ui.secGochara: The sky right now — this week & the months ahead
ui.careerCaveat: These are inclinations the tradition reads, offered to reflect on — not a prediction of any particular job.
ui.dashaIntro: Vimshottari daśā — a 120-year cycle. Which lord is running is a fact of your Moon; the theme beside it is what the tradition associates with that lord. Without your exact minute the dates drift a little, so read them as close guides.
ui.dashaIntroNoTime: Your daśā keys off the Moon, so it works without a birth time (dates to the year).
ui.mangalAbsent: In your chart, Mars does not fall in a Manglik house from the Lagna, Moon or Venus — so this dosha is not formed.
ui.remediesIntro: Offered as calm, optional heritage practices — never required, never a cure, and never a substitute for real-world care.
ui.vargasIntro: The Navāṁśa (D9) and Daśāṁśa (D10) magnify marriage/dharma and career. Planets in these are robust; the D9/D10 ascendant is sensitive to your exact minute of birth.
ui.timewarn: Without your birth time we can't place your Ascendant, houses, career or divisional charts. What's below — your Moon, nakshatra, the day's planetary signs and your daśā — is genuinely time-robust and shown honestly. Add your birth time above to unlock the full reading.

tpl.ayan: Sidereal zodiac · Lahiri ayanamsa {deg} · whole-sign houses · IST (UTC+5:30)
tpl.ayanNoTime: Sidereal · Lahiri ayanamsa {deg} · positions at local noon (time unknown)
tpl.persLagna: Your rising sign (Lagna) is {rashi} — in the tradition this gives {trait}
tpl.persLord: Its ruler, {graha}, sits in your {ord} house — the house of {houseSig} — which is where the tradition sees your life-energy naturally flowing.
tpl.persMoon: Your Moon rests in {nakshatra}, a birth-star of {traits}
tpl.career10th: Your 10th house — of career and public action — falls in {rashi}, ruled by {graha}. The tradition associates {graha} with work in {career}.
tpl.careerInTenth: With {planets} in your 10th house, your work leans further toward {keywords}.
tpl.careerAmk: Your Amatyakaraka — the ‘career minister’ of the Jaimini system — is {graha}, pointing toward {career}.
tpl.careerD10: In your Daśāṁśa (D10), the divisional chart of career, the ascendant falls in {rashi} (ruled by {graha}) — another thread in your professional signature.
tpl.gocharaJupiter: Guru (Jupiter) is transiting your {ord} from the Moon — now in {rashi}. {text}
tpl.gocharaMoon: This week the Moon is passing through {rashi} — {text}. The Moon shifts sign every couple of days, so this is the fast, everyday tide.
tpl.mangalPresent: In your chart Mars falls in a Manglik position from {refs}. {calm}
tpl.lucky: These follow your Lagna lord, {graha} ({keyword}) — the traditional associations that are held to support you.

mangalCancel.ownSign: Mars is in its own sign, which tradition holds strongly softens the dosha
mangalCancel.exalted: Mars is exalted, which tradition holds strongly softens the dosha
mangalCancel.withJupiter: Mars is joined by Jupiter, a classical cancellation
mangalCancel.withMoon: Mars is joined by the Moon, which tradition counts as easing

lagnaTraits: a pioneering, energetic and direct nature — quick to begin, courageous, happiest when leading from the front.
a steady, patient and sensual nature — value comfort, beauty and security, and build slowly but surely.
a curious, communicative and versatile nature — quick-witted, restless, and gifted with words and ideas.
a caring, sensitive and protective nature — deeply tied to home, family and memory, with a strong inner tide.
a warm, dignified and generous nature — natural warmth and pride, drawn to recognition and creative expression.
an analytical, precise and service-minded nature — practical, discerning, and happiest when useful and organised.
a graceful, fair-minded and relational nature — drawn to harmony, partnership, art and balance.
an intense, private and determined nature — deep feeling, strong will, and a gift for regeneration and research.
an optimistic, philosophical and freedom-loving nature — a seeker of meaning, truth, travel and higher learning.
a disciplined, ambitious and enduring nature — patient, responsible, and built for the long climb.
an original, humane and independent nature — forward-looking, unconventional, and moved by ideals and community.
a compassionate, imaginative and gentle nature — intuitive, artistic, and quietly drawn to the spiritual and the boundless.
house: the self, body, temperament and the way you meet the world
wealth, family, speech and what you value
home, mother, land, vehicles and inner peace
intelligence, creativity, children, romance and learning
daily work, health, service, and the overcoming of obstacles
partnership, marriage, and dealings with others
transformation, depth, longevity and hidden matters
fortune, dharma, father, teachers and long journeys
career, status, action in the world and public standing
gains, income, friendships and aspirations fulfilled
seclusion, letting-go, foreign lands and the inner life

Su.career: government, administration, leadership, medicine, politics or any field of authority and public standing
Su.remedy: offering water to the rising Sun at dawn and honouring one's father
Su.dashaTheme: authority, recognition, vitality and matters of the father and the self
Su.do: lead with integrity and let your work be seen
Su.dont: let pride or ego harden into stubbornness

Mo.career: public-facing work, hospitality, care and nursing, food and dairy, travel, or anything to do with the mind and the public
Mo.remedy: honouring the mother and keeping a gentle Monday observance
Mo.dashaTheme: emotions, home, the mother, public life and the ebb and flow of the mind
Mo.do: tend your inner peace and your close relationships
Mo.dont: let passing moods make lasting decisions

Ma.career: engineering, the armed forces or police, surgery, sport, real estate, or work involving metal, machines and fire
Ma.remedy: reciting the Hanuman Chalisa on Tuesdays and channelling energy into effort, not conflict
Ma.dashaTheme: energy, courage, drive, competition and matters of land and siblings
Ma.do: put your drive into disciplined, worthwhile effort
Ma.dont: act in anger or rush into needless conflict

Me.career: business and trade, writing and media, accounts and analysis, communication, technology or teaching
Me.remedy: supporting students, keeping your word, and green offerings on Wednesday
Me.dashaTheme: intellect, commerce, learning, speech and adaptability
Me.do: learn, write and put your ideas to practical use
Me.dont: scatter your focus or bend the truth for convenience

Ju.career: teaching, law, finance and banking, counselling, academia, priesthood or advisory work
Ju.mantra: Om Gram Grim Grom Sah Gurave Namaha
Ju.remedy: respecting teachers and elders and quiet charity on Thursdays
Ju.dashaTheme: wisdom, growth, fortune, teachers, children and dharma
Ju.do: seek knowledge and give generously of what you know
Ju.dont: let optimism become excess or dogma

Ve.career: the arts, music and film, design and fashion, beauty, luxury goods, or hospitality and relationships
Ve.remedy: honouring the women in your life and white offerings on Friday
Ve.dashaTheme: love, beauty, comfort, relationships and the arts
Ve.do: cultivate beauty, art and warm relationships
Ve.dont: let comfort and pleasure drift into indulgence

Sa.career: long-term institutions, service and labour, law, agriculture, mining and oil, or any patient, structural work
Sa.remedy: serving the elderly and the needy and quiet Saturday service
Sa.dashaTheme: discipline, patience, karma, endurance and hard-won maturity
Sa.do: be patient, honest and steady — Saturn rewards the long road
Sa.dont: cut corners or shirk your responsibilities

Ra.career: technology, aviation, research, foreign lands and cultures, media, or unconventional and pioneering fields
Ra.mantra: Om Bhram Bhrim Bhraum Sah Rahave Namaha
Ra.remedy: keeping cleanliness, honouring Goddess Durga, and staying grounded
Ra.dashaTheme: ambition, foreign connections, sudden change and unconventional paths
Ra.do: aim high but keep one foot firmly on the ground
Ra.dont: chase shortcuts or let obsession blur your judgement

Ke.career: research and investigation, spirituality and healing, technology, medicine or the occult
Ke.mantra: Om Sram Srim Sraum Sah Ketave Namaha
Ke.remedy: honouring Lord Ganesha and cultivating a quiet inner practice
Ke.dashaTheme: detachment, insight, spirituality and the resolving of old karma
Ke.do: trust your intuition and pursue depth over surface
Ke.dont: withdraw so far that you neglect the practical

mangal.intro: In the tradition, Mangal (Kuja / Manglik) Dosha is read when Mars sits in the 1st, 2nd, 4th, 7th, 8th or 12th house counted from the Lagna, the Moon or Venus. It is classically associated with extra fire and independence in relationships and a call for patience in marriage — and it is one of the most over-dramatised ideas in popular astrology.
mangal.calm: Importantly, the classical texts record many cancellations (bhanga) that soften or nullify it entirely, and it is not a verdict on your marriage. It is offered here as heritage, not as fate.

kaalSarp.intro: Kaal Sarp yoga is read when all seven grahas are hemmed on one side of the Rahu–Ketu axis. Tradition associates it with an intense, effort-driven life whose rewards often arrive after a struggle — and, like Manglik, it is far more nuanced than popular fear suggests, with many mitigating placements.
kaalSarp.absent: Your seven grahas are not fully hemmed within the Rahu–Ketu axis, so a full Kaal Sarp yoga is not formed.

sadeSati.intro: Sade Sati is the roughly seven-and-a-half-year passage of Saturn (Shani) through the 12th, 1st and 2nd signs from your natal Moon. Tradition treats it as a demanding but deeply maturing teacher — a time of responsibility, honesty and slow, real growth — not a punishment.
sadeSati.rising: You are in the first (rising) phase — Saturn in the 12th from your Moon. Tradition reads this as a time of endings, inward turning and releasing what no longer serves.
sadeSati.peak: You are in the peak phase — Saturn over your natal Moon. Tradition reads this as the most testing but transformative stretch, asking for patience, discipline and honest self-work.
sadeSati.setting: You are in the third (setting) phase — Saturn in the 2nd from your Moon. Tradition reads this as consolidation: steadying finances, family and speech as the passage eases.
sadeSati.dhaiya: Saturn is transiting the 4th or 8th from your Moon — the shorter 'dhaiya' (Kantaka / Ashtama Shani). Tradition reads this as a smaller, focused period of responsibility touching home or transformation.
sadeSati.none: Saturn is not currently in the 12th, 1st or 2nd from your Moon, so you are not in Sade Sati at this time.

gochara.jupiterGoodText: Jupiter (Guru) is transiting a supportive house from your Moon — tradition reads this as a favourable, growth-bringing influence over the coming months, good for learning, opportunity and goodwill.
gochara.jupiterHardText: Jupiter (Guru) is transiting a more mixed house from your Moon — tradition counsels patience and care with commitments for a while, and trusting the longer arc of its ~year-long stay in each sign.
gochara.chandraGoodText: the Moon is passing through a favourable house from your birth Moon — a lighter, more supportive few days for mood and initiative
gochara.chandraHardText: the Moon is passing through a more sensitive house from your birth Moon — a good few days to rest and go gently, as the mood shifts every couple of days
gochara.note: These are the actual sidereal positions of the planets right now; the reading of them is Jyotish tradition.
